name: match
description: Full competitive-match lifecycle inside the Sports domain. Answers 'who played whom, when, where, with which lineup, and what was the result?'. Ships the four-stage state machine (SCHEDULED -> LINEUP_SET -> IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED; plus POSTPONED / CANCELLED / ABANDONED off-happy-paths) that drives squad selection, formation-slotted lineup, publish gates with parent-facing notifications, in-progress scoring, W/D/L result recording via the sport registry's scoring strategy (mechanism #4), man-of-match awards, in-match events (goals / cards / substitutions / apparatus scores / lane times), coach recap notes with visibility tiers, opponent-logo cache keyed by normalized name for reuse across matches, and multi-team support for galas + festivals + swim meets where a single match aggregates N participants instead of a home/away pair. Sport-agnostic by construction — every score/time/distance/apparatus dimension flows through the sport registry's scoring strategy so football, swimming, gymnastics, and combat sports share one match model with different scoring outputs. Depends on `sports::registry` for the scoring strategy resolver, `sports::season` for temporal scoping, `sports::athlete` + `sports::athlete-enrollment` for squad eligibility (only enrolled athletes appear in the squad picker), `sports::attendance` (Wave 3+ for post-match auto-attendance from the lineup), `sports::formations` for formation-slot codes, `sports::event` for the invitation + publish + notify surface, `platform::facility` + `platform::branch` for the venue. Owns seven tables: `matches` (canonical fixture row), `match_participants` (polymorphic entries for multi-team meets), `match_squad_entries` (athlete-to-match roster with role + shirt + formation slot), `match_results` (per-participant final score / time / distance / apparatus + outcome), `match_events` (in-match timeline: goals, cards, substitutions, apparatus scores), `match_notes` (coach recap with visibility tiers), `opponent_logos` (cache keyed by normalized opponent name for cross-match reuse + admin search). Every row is tenant-scoped; NO row carries application_id, region_id, organization_id, or scope_node_id — cascaded through the tenant. `MATCH_RESULT_PUBLISHED`, `LINEUP_PUBLISHED`, `MATCH_POSTPONED`, `MATCH_ABANDONED`, `MOTM_AWARDED` events feed downstream reporting (competition standings recompute, parent + public site notifications, attendance auto-materialisation, badge / achievement grants).
planned_consumers: competition (Wave 2b — aggregates match results into standings; consumes MatchResultPublished)
reporting (Wave 6 — season/branch/team performance rollups over the match ledger)
notifications (Wave 5 — dispatches MatchLineupPublished + MatchResultPublished to families + public site)
attendance (Wave 3+ — auto-materialises attendance rows from the published lineup on MatchStarted)
awards (Wave 3+ — badge / MOTM streak grants on MatchMotmAwarded)
development (Wave 3+ — reads match_events for skill / drill progression evidence)

wraps.rationale: Pure domain module — no third-party integrations wrapped. Scoring strategy resolution is provided by the sports registry (mechanism #4); this module consumes it via `ScoringStrategyResolver` binding.

tenant.description: Tenant-facing competitive-match surface. Every route carries the `sanctum` guard + `X-Application-Id` header + `match.enforce_lineup_publish_gate` middleware on the publish-lineup endpoint. Head coach + admin + owner drive the state machine; coach role builds squads + logs events; parent + public site read published lineups + results only via visibility narrowing.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant support tooling. Ops + support read (never mutate) matches across tenants for support-ticket triage + moderation of publicly visible content.
